notes.anchor: M. CICERO S. D. P. SILIO PROPR.
notes.note: The letter-heading; one of a cluster of recommendations (Fam. 13.61-65) to P. Silius Nerva, propraetor of Bithynia and Pontus in 51-50 BC. "S. D." abbreviates salutem dicit, "sends greetings"; PROPR. = propraetore, governing with praetorian power.

notes.anchor: to clear up whatever business he has in your province
notes.note: P. Silius's province was Bithynia and Pontus, on the southern shore of the Black Sea; Laenius had affairs there that needed the governor's intervention to settle. Cicero's request that he be sent home "free and at his own disposal" implies the business was litigation or a debt that tied him down.
